hints.vocative: The vocative would be 'Deus' (or 'Dee' rarely); here it is the subject

hints.1st declension: 1st declension nouns end in -a in the nominative
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension nouns have varied nominative endings

hints.to gather: That would be the infinitive of 'colligere'
hints.to inhabit: That would be the infinitive of 'habitare'
hints.to believe: That would be the infinitive of 'credere'

hints.colligo, colligere, collegi, collectum: These are the principal parts of 'colligo' (to collect)
hints.adoro, adorare, adoravi, adoratum: These are the principal parts of 'adoro' (to worship/adore)
hints.veneror, venerari, veneratus sum, —: These are the principal parts of 'veneror' (to revere)

hints.to have power: That would be closer to 'valere'; 'posse' means 'to be able'
hints.to wish: That would be the infinitive of 'velle'
hints.to dare: That would be the infinitive of 'audere'

steps.reveal: potest = he/it is able / he can

hints.sum, esse, fui, futurus: These are the principal parts of 'sum' (to be)
hints.valeo, valere, valui, valitum: These are the principal parts of 'valeo' (to be strong)
hints.queo, quire, quivi, quitum: These are the principal parts of 'queo' (to be able, archaic)

hints.to destroy: That would be the infinitive of 'delere'
hints.to free: That would be the infinitive of 'liberare'
hints.to lead out: That would be the infinitive of 'educere'

hints.rapio, rapere, rapui, raptum: These are the principal parts of 'rapio' (to seize)
hints.capio, capere, cepi, captum: These are the principal parts of 'capio' (to take)
hints.libero, liberare, liberavi, liberatum: These are the principal parts of 'libero' (to free)

hints.nominative: 'Nos' can be nominative, but here it is the direct object of 'eripere'
hints.ablative: 'Nos' can be ablative with a preposition, but here it is accusative

translation: Our God whom we worship is able to rescue us
